items.headline: Create Medicines raises $122M to bolster in vivo CAR-T therapies
items.preview: Create Medicines has secured $122 million in funding to enhance its cell therapy capabilities.
items.article:
Cambridge-based Create Medicines announced Thursday it has raised another $122 million to bolster its cell therapy capabilities across cancers and autoimmune conditions.
The Series B financing round was co-led by existing investors Arch Venture Partners, Newpath Partners and Hatteras Venture Partners. Create is developing cell therapies that reprogram immune cells inside the body, known as 'in vivo.' This innovative approach offers a 'one-day manufacturing process' that traditional cell therapies cannot provide.
Why this matters: The funding will support the development of therapies that could significantly improve treatment options for patients with various cancers and autoimmune diseases. Create has already dosed more than 50 patients across its programs, establishing a substantial clinical dataset in the field.

items.headline: Lupus Awareness Month meets Women’s Health Month: Advancing care, research, and advocacy
items.preview: May is recognized as both Lupus Awareness Month and Women’s Health Month, highlighting the need for better lupus care.
items.article:
May is both Lupus Awareness Month and Women’s Health Month, and the timing is significant as lupus affects female patients at a higher rate than men. The Lupus Foundation of America (LFA) is leading efforts to improve the lives of those affected by lupus through research, advocacy, and education.
Why this matters: The LFA's initiatives aim to raise awareness and funding for lupus research, which is crucial for the estimated 1.5 million Americans living with the disease. The foundation has generated over $795 million in federal research funding in the last five years, emphasizing the importance of continued advocacy and support.

items.headline: Investigating the causes of rheumatoid arthritis pain
items.preview: Researchers are exploring the neural basis of rheumatoid arthritis pain using innovative techniques.
items.article:
Researchers from the Karolinska Institutet are investigating the causes of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) pain, which is not solely explained by inflammation. They utilized Adeno Associated Virus (AAV) products to uncover the neural basis of inflammatory pain in mouse models.
Why this matters: This study identifies specific cytokine and signaling pathways responsible for joint pain in RA, paving the way for targeted therapies that could provide effective pain relief for patients suffering from this debilitating condition.

items.headline: Millions for Preclinical Immunology: Boehringer Teams Up with Immunitas
items.preview: Boehringer Ingelheim has partnered with Immunitas for a preclinical antibody program worth up to €407.5m.
items.article:
Boehringer Ingelheim is investing in next-generation autoimmune therapies through a deal with Immunitas Therapeutics, potentially worth up to €407.5 million. This collaboration focuses on a preclinical antibody designed to selectively eliminate immune cells involved in chronic inflammatory disorders.
Why this matters: The agreement underscores the competitive landscape of selective immune-cell depletion approaches, which could lead to more effective treatments for autoimmune diseases, enhancing patient outcomes and expanding therapeutic options.

items.headline: Acoustic delivery of indocyanine green via biosynthetic gas vesicles for tumor photothermal therapy
items.preview: A novel method using biosynthetic gas vesicles enhances tumor targeting for photothermal therapy.
items.article:
Researchers have developed biosynthetic gas vesicles (GVs) conjugated with indocyanine green (ICG) for enhanced delivery in tumor photothermal therapy. This method improves the stability and circulation of ICG, allowing for effective tumor targeting and treatment.
Why this matters: This innovative approach could significantly improve the efficacy of photothermal therapy, providing a promising strategy for cancer treatment that minimizes toxicity and enhances patient outcomes.
